notes: Subject of this profile. Filed for bankruptcy January 14, 2009. Assets sold: wireless to Ericsson ($1.13B), enterprise to Avaya ($915M), optical/Ethernet to Ciena ($774M), carrier VoIP to GENBAND ($282M), patents to Apple/Microsoft/Ericsson/RIM/EMC/Sony consortium ($4.5B 2011). Peak market cap $297-400B (~2000). At bankruptcy, ~30,000 employees; peak was 95,000.
Cited as Nortel's primary hardware competitor. Cisco dominated IP routing while Nortel focused on ATM. Cisco survived and became the dominant enterprise networking vendor; Nortel did not.
Cited as competitor. Spun off from AT&T in 1996. Merged with Alcatel in 2006; Nokia acquired Alcatel-Lucent in 2016.
Cited as competitor in enterprise networking market.
Nortel partner for next-generation multimedia client-server telephone development. Sun acquired by Oracle in 2010.
Acquired Nortel's enterprise solutions for $915M in December 2009. Avaya itself filed for bankruptcy twice (2017, 2023).
Acquired Nortel's wireless infrastructure assets for $1.13B in November 2009.
Acquired Nortel's optical networking and carrier Ethernet businesses for $774M in March 2010.
